description: Step 2 of T-012's 4-step CSP roadmap. T-012 Step 1 (per-request nonce middleware + nonced script/style tags) landed in commit 6d1d9de. **Progress (commit 6e1ba5b):** Infrastructure + 103/364 handlers converted (28%). - `static/js/actions.js` shipped — single delegated listener for click/change/input/submit, dispatches by `data-="fnName"` attribute. Loads before notifications.js in base.html. - 103 zero-arg handlers across 26 templates converted via a strict regex pass: only the simple `()` form was touched. Migration script left at `/tmp/convert_zeroarg_handlers.py`. **Remaining (262 handlers):** Each needs case-by-case rewrite. Patterns to expect (sampled): - `onclick="foo({{ var }})"` — Jinja interpolation. Convert to `data-click="foo" data-arg="{{ var }}"`, action reads `el.dataset.arg`. - `onclick="foo(this)"` — `this` reference. The new dispatcher already passes the element (`fn.call(el, e)`), so the function just reads its first arg (the event) or `this`. - `onclick="event.stopPropagation(); foo()"` — multi-statement. Convert to a thin wrapper in actions.js or extract a named function. - `onclick="foo(); bar()"` — multi-call. Same pattern: extract a wrapper. - `` — really common pattern for broken-image fallback. Could centralize as `data-on-error="hide"` in actions.js. **The right pattern for arg-bearing handlers:** ```html ``` ```js window.loadFitting = function(e) { var id = parseInt(this.dataset.fittingId, 10); // ... existing logic }; ``` The migration is mostly mechanical-but-not-regex-automatable: every site needs to be read, args extracted, function signature adjusted to read from `this.dataset` / `e`. Best done per-template or per-feature in dedicated sessions. **Done when:** browser devtools shows 0 CSP violations for inline event handlers across the major routes (dashboard, fitting tool, star map, admin, intel, structure timers). Then T-032/T-033 can flip enforcing.
title: CSP Step 2: refactor 364 inline event handlers to addEventListener (VVP-2026-007)
